** **Now for the bad news:** We could lose it all if Democrats don’t choose the right candidate to go up against Ron Johnson in the general election. And if the last decade of Wisconsin politics has taught us anything, it’s that national Republicans will stop at nothing to try and keep this seat red.

This is one of the most competitive races in the country. Insider Elections and Cook Political Report has this race rated as a toss up, so we can't afford to take our foot off the gas right now.

Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell met with GOP megadonors earlier this year to figure out how to take back the Senate. Protecting their friend Ron Johnson is at the top of their list.

_**That’s why I urgently need your help today.**_

I truly believe to beat Ron Johnson and his right-wing extremist friends in November we need a strong democratic candidate who is:
* Putting in serious facetime with voters
* Building solid and lasting relationships across the entire state
* And running a campaign that brings Wisconsin together

Thankfully, this is exactly what I've been doing over the course of this campaign.

Alex Lasry

Peter: Democrat Abby Finkenauer – who is the youngest woman to ever flip a U.S. House seat from red to blue – launched her campaign for Iowa's Senate seat to defeat Chuck Grassley and protect our Democratic Senate majority.** But Abby's up against a tight midnight fundraising deadline and doesn't yet have the resources it will take to flip Iowa blue. Here's where things stand:**
Defeating Sen. Grassley in Iowa means Democrats are virtually guaranteed to expand our majority in the Senate, and that's exactly why Mitch McConnell and his allies will spend whatever it takes to keep Iowa red. **And right now, Senate Republicans have more than $44 million in the bank that they can shell out at a moment's notice to attack Abby.**
With only 27% of Iowa voters saying they want to reelect Sen. name: Val Demings
subject: Forever grateful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
Today on Memorial Day, we remember the thousands of Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our nation.
| | |
---
Friend,

Today on Memorial Day, we remember the thousands of Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our nation. We can never give them the limitless thanks that they have earned for their bravery, but we can honor them and what they died for.
After 27 years of dedicated service as a police officer, I know what it's like to put on a uniform each morning – ready to risk your life because you took an oath to protect and serve your community. So I know that the work our service members do each day is tough work. It's brave work. And it's work that results in a lot of sleepless nights and sacrifices – not only them, but for their loved ones at home as well.
My heart is with the families of these fallen heroes, whose courage and devotion is worthy of our deepest appreciation. We are forever grateful.
From me and my family to you and yours, I hope you have a safe and meaningful Memorial Day.
Val Demings

Friend, I have an urgent update:
**My opponent, Herschel Walker (R), has raised more than $15 MILLION to defeat me and take back the Senate for Republicans.**
**Even worse, Mitch McConnell's super PAC is gearing up to blanket the airwaves in Georgia with a new, MULTI-MILLION dollar ad buy. You can bet the GOP will attack me with everything they have.**
With polls showing me 1 point down, this surge in Republican spending could put us at a major disadvantage. Senate control is at stake in this race – and if we fail to fight back now, we could fall even further behind.
**That’s why we set a goal to raise $100,000 before tomorrow’s crucial end-of-month deadline so we can pull ahead and WIN. But time is running out, and we're still coming up short. Please, friend, I'm counting on you:**

name: Imani Oakley
subject: The fraught history of Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Oakley for Congress
body:
Dear Peter,
Today, we celebrate Memorial Day and honor those who lost their lives fighting in the military.
Memorial Day has a fraught history. It started as a tradition among former enslaved people, celebrating emancipation and honoring those who fought for it.
Today, it’s too often used as a day to glorify our country’s history of unjust, for-profit wars.
In my family, we’ve always understood the contradiction of this holiday. Thirteen of my family members — all Black men — have served in the military, one of whom never returned home.
All but two of those men served while our family continued to suffer as second class citizens under Jim Crow.
That is, sadly, the history of war in our country — the contradiction of young people of color fighting for an ideal of western freedom abroad that they’ve been continually denied at home.
I join with other military families in mourning the loss of so many service members.
But, on this Memorial Day, I’m also committed to continuing my fight to end unjust wars that pit people of color against people of color and only benefit the predominantly white political class — to pursue freedom based on ballots and not bullets.
Thank you to all who served and to all who made the ultimate sacrifice fighting for our freedom. I hope that together, we can put a stop to for-profit violence and realize the promise of true freedom for all people.
With gratitude,
Imani
Paid for by Oakley for Congress
Oakley for Congress
PO Box 1181
Montclair, NJ 07042
United States

name: Tom Nelson
subject: Wishing you a Happy Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nelson for Wisconsin
body:
This Memorial Day, I want to express my appreciation for all of the brave heroes who have lost their lives in service to our country. We are all eternally grateful for their sacrifice and will never forget their tireless commitment to our freedom.

This day has special meaning for all Americans, but I would like to give special recognition to the family and friends of our fallen soldiers. Your resilience in the face of great loss has inspired myself and so many others to work to make our country a better place.

I also want to implore you to continue supporting our veterans every day, in any way you can. However you celebrate today, please remember to give thanks for our freedom.

Wishing you all a safe, healthy, and happy Memorial Day.

Tom
Fifth-generation Wisconsinite and Lutheran pastor son Tom Nelson works overtime, while raising his family, to defend hard-working families in the fight against corporate politicians and Foxconn-like special interests. As your U.S. senator, he’ll relentlessly fight a corrupt system in DC to promote Main Street solutions to our rigged economy and the existential threat of climate change. Tom has won six elections in a Trump county because he gets things done, including saving 300 union jobs at a hometown paper mill that was going to be shut down. Learn about his riveting story here [https://madison.com/ct/opinion/column/john_nichols/john-nichols-tom-nelsons-urgent-argument-for-a-politics-of-social-solidarity/article_2dc75be4-ea6f-5adb-8015-cba03aeabd6c.html] . name: Deb Baldus McGrath
subject: A Memorial Day message
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEB MCGRATH FOR CONGRESS
body:
They made the ultimate sacrifice.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45898546/347992748/-1740198439?amount=25&refcode=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ms=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzk0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74mbqOMcQGxXW36W6NZi3oY0Yel7QRMPfsLVRqo6P1Y=) Hello Peter,

None of us here today can know what it means to give your life for our country. But as a veteran myself, I honor their stories. I’d like to share some that are particularly meaningful to me and my family with you.

During the Korean conflict, my father served with a man named Willy. Long hours on patrol and in the supply tent made them fast friends. One night, Willy was killed while on patrol, making the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country.

My father treasured a picture of them together for the rest of his life. Willy’s final act of devotion ensured that his story was never forgotten in our family. Devotion to our country is my inspiration for joining the military. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45898550/347992759/-473890518?refcode=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ms=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzk0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74mbqOMcQGxXW36W6NZi3oY0Yel7QRMPfsLVRqo6P1Y=&emci=511b22bc-e7da-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=5944225) _Deb's father Al and his best friend, Willy_ In my own service, I have lost friends in the line of duty. I will never forget their courage and conviction. Knowing them, and understanding their deep patriotism, inspires me to never give up fighting for our democracy. Though their names are known to few, their stories will live forever. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45898554/347992762/-473890518?refcode=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ms=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzk0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74mbqOMcQGxXW36W6NZi3oY0Yel7QRMPfsLVRqo6P1Y=&emci=511b22bc-e7da-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=5944225) _The CIA Memorial Wall_ Across the nearly 250 years since our country declared independence, men and women have given everything to make sure America could remain free and our people could be safe. At every turn, brave service members have paid with their lives to protect this country, their fellow servicemembers, and citizens at home.

I’m a veteran. So is my husband. So was my dad. Our son is serving now. We take an oath to protect and defend the Constitution against all enemies, and that means meeting the enemy.

Every day, I feel a great responsibility to remember the lives and service of those who pay the ultimate sacrifice. And we will never forget prisoners of war and those still missing in action. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45898564/347992768/-473890518?refcode=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ms=ea.e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzk0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlODlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=74mbqOMcQGxXW36W6NZi3oY0Yel7QRMPfsLVRqo6P1Y=&emci=511b22bc-e7da-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=e89fa3d7-20e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=5944225) _Deb at a POW MIA table in Milwaukee_ This Memorial Day, I hope you’ll take a moment to reflect and honor the sacrifice of the heroes who have made America the land of the free and the home of the brave.

In remembrance,

Deb | |
---

clean_body: They made the ultimate sacrifice. | = Hello Peter, None of us here today can know what it means to give your life for our country. But as a veteran myself, I honor their stories. I’d like to share some that are particularly meaningful to me and my family with you. During the Korean conflict, my father served with a man named Willy. Long hours on patrol and in the supply tent made them fast friends. One night, Willy was killed while on patrol, making the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country. name: Patty Pansing Brooks
subject: A call for peace
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PATTY FOR CONGRESS
body:
My whole heart and deepest sympathies are with all who are remembering loved ones today.

|
---
| | Dear friends, On this Memorial Day, I am reflecting on our world and the loved ones whom we have lost. As I write this message, our brothers and sisters in Ukraine have crossed the three-month mark fighting for their lives. _Three months_ of fighting to protect their communities — and their democracy — from hateful, violent attacks. Here at home, hundreds of thousands of families are profoundly touched by grief and the repercussions of violence. We mourn alongside the communities of the brave people who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedom, and loved ones who have been lost to senseless violence. **Today and _every_ day, we honor our nation’s grieving families by working toward peace.** My whole heart and deepest sympathies are with all who are remembering loved ones today. We will forever share in your grief. Together, Patty Pansing Brooks
---

name: Jeff Ettinger
subject: My thoughts on Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ETTINGER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ETTINGER FOR CONGRESS

| Hello,

On Memorial Day, we remember that freedom is not free.

When we’ve needed them, men and women in uniform have always answered the call to defend our country and secure our liberty. Across our whole history, so many have made the ultimate sacrifice so that the rest of us could know peace.

The servicemembers who laid down their lives for America represent the very best of us: their selflessness, bravery, and patriotism deserve to be remembered.

Most of us will never understand what it means to give everything for our country. But our duty to the fallen is to honor their lives, their service, and their sacrifice — and not only on Memorial Day.

I will always try to do just that.

\- Jeff |
| PAID FOR BY ETTINGER FOR CONGRESS
---

PO BOX 741

subject: How we defeat Doug Mastriano:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
**Trump's endorsed candidate Doug Mastriano won Pennsylvania's primary.**

Peter, Doug Mastriano is a right-wing extremist who traveled to the January 6th insurrection. _He could be the next leader of Pennsylvania._

Pennsylvania's races have often come down to less than a percentage point. So if we're going to defeat Mastriano, we need to compete with their MAGA money.
[**We need your urgent support to help elect Josh Shapiro. We're setting an ambitious goal of raising $10,000 before our May fundraising deadline tomorrow to elect Pennsylvania Democrats. name: Gary Sinise
subject: The National Memorial Day Parade is back

| | | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z9i933lg69ovc13dj67go2b4hfhjtj7mdvv76b18_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
Fellow American, It’s Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like _Apollo 13_ and _The Green Mile_ — but the role that changed my life, and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, was as Lt. Dan in _Forrest Gump_. **This year, I’m honored to again serve as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C.** The National Memorial Day Parade is an event like no other ... and this year, the American Veterans Center is bringing it BACK in full force, with 5,000+ participants marching down Constitution Avenue before a crowd of 250,000+ in-person, as well as T.V. broadcast that reaches 100 million households around the country. [**Fellow American, the National Memorial Day Parade is PRIMARILY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous Americans like you. Will you help chip in today to help support the Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zk643fpqgmeb2itj79cm7tc23j256edh2r1tbj3o_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
[**Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zjciu8kslaek41qep7mgoe7mqkavvs87kqkihirg_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zkpst8rfug7vovmi8ahke4mgnsdup7e0o1kfr4t0_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
When the American Veterans Center first called me more than 15 years ago and invited me to the National Memorial Day Parade, I was honored to join the event to remember our fallen heroes. The American Veterans Center revived the Parade in 2005 after it had been dormant for nearly 70 years. Now, in 2022, they’re bringing the Parade back to its full-scale glory after two years of pandemic precautions. Every American should be proud that generations of our brave servicemen and servicewomen, from the last of our “Greatest Generation” heroes of World War II to our veterans from Afghanistan and Iraq, will get the public thanks for their service that they deserve this year. But I know that the defenders and families of the fallen who will join me in the National Memorial Day Parade will be thinking of their friends and loved ones who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country — the brave Americans we honor on Memorial Day. **Sadly, our country is losing hundreds of World War II veterans each day, so the mission of the National Memorial Day Parade is _more important now than ever_ — as this year’s event may be the LAST opportunity for our WWII heroes to commemorate their friends who never came home.** [**Please make your most generous contribution of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, to support the National Memorial Day Parade and make it the tribute that our nation’s heroes deserve.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zg7i0fqukagt0tp50pn1p49aub701ku796q5v420_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
[**Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zd4lrfceqbq4r0obt3m0vrhs11lqqb451r9k6u60_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
The National Memorial Day Parade costs a fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that’s contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like:
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever.
* Hold our National Youth Summit which allows young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, and Vietnam.
* Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z5emomad12n5h78ofr9e6e2o1cu45onutt7lmeg8_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
[**Please join me in support of the National Memorial Day Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, or more right now.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z2belqml69578rrvjnuknll4tq8bgh86s623ehlg_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs) But no matter how much you can give to this important cause, I want to ask something additional of you.

On Memorial Day — this year and every year — please join me in honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms. Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in D.C. Visit a local veterans’ cemetery. Send a note to a family of the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country. _I believe we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, but we can always do a little more._ Fellow American, thank you for your support of America's heroes, and please — [**if you're able, make a gift to help fund the National Memorial Day Parade right now.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zapcrpjns5dn7oj37udfpem9594d6sgbt9igtqug_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
Sincerely,

Gary Sinise
Honorary Grand Marshal
National Memorial Day Parade
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zasn4mv3bibt0sv6ra0hedovdv7468r7hqs66r70_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---
[**Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zruks739mss6oje1mvm1jlhob5j1kl0er67nl2g8_rp22sh2s8i66pb6cji69oj2cph2bs)
---

---

A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. © American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH |
body:
Today, I join all Americans in honoring the service members who have made the ultimate sacrifice to keep us safe, secure, and free.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45899091/347994072/-947982778?amounts=3,10,25,50,100&refcode=mh-jtk1101-ot-fl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMzlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IwtzAKn1cVHGdJExeFBzDR40TyksTrUBrWPimXyFQVs=)
---
Hello,
Today, I join all Americans in honoring the service members who have made the ultimate sacrifice to keep us safe, secure, and free.
These fallen service members are examples of the essence of what it means to be an American — to be willing to give your life for the cause of freedom and for those who you have never met simply because they are part of this democracy too.
I know that today, many of us are remembering friends, family, or fellow service members who gave their lives to protect our freedom. We owe each and every one of them an enormous debt, and we bear the responsibility of honoring their sacrifice today and every day.
We can honor that sacrifice by supporting the families and communities they leave behind, and ensuring that our surviving service members have the infrastructure and care they need both in service and when they return to civilian life.
I hope that you'll join me and take a moment today to think about the Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country.
With every good wish,

Maggie Hassan
| If you'd like to support Maggie Hassan for Senate, **[donate now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45899092/347994085/-1829049351?refcode=mh-jtk1101-ot-fl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMzlmYTNkNy0yMGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IwtzAKn1cVHGdJExeFBzDR40TyksTrUBrWPimXyFQVs=)**. name: Nan Whaley
subject: a humble request
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Whaley/Stephens
body:
It’s going to take all of us standing together and building a movement that will last.

Team, I’m running for governor of Ohio because I believe it’s time for a change.

The same corrupt crew has been running the show in Columbus for far too long now. I know that we can take back this state and get it working again for our families, not the corporate backers.

But it’s going to take all of us standing together and building a movement that will last.

**[My team just let me know that we still need to raise $5,864 online by midnight tomorrow to meet our May fundraising goal. So I’m asking: will you pitch in $5 right now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45900728/348000591/-1664405127?refcode=nw_em_220530_1_t2&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZDhkZmFlYS0yMmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pOZ_w9XWFHcePwEk2T1tisCwLID1HT_QXw8oKYRCrLk=)**
As the mayor of Dayton, I saw some of Ohio’s biggest challenges up close and personal. From the devastating opioid crisis to the COVID-19 pandemic to a mass shooting in our city, Dayton has been a microcosm of what so many communities are experiencing across the state.

Peter, I wanted to take this opportunity to wish you and your family a blessed Memorial Day. Today is a day of reflection and remembrance as we honor the courage and sacrifice of our fallen veterans. Today, our freedom is ours because of the heroes who dedicated their lives to protecting it. We are forever in debt to those who paid the ultimate price for our liberty. Please join me in taking time to reflect today. Sincerely, Guy Ciarrocchi

clean_body: Have a blessed Memorial Day | Peter, I wanted to take this opportunity to wish you and your family a blessed Memorial Day. Today is a day of reflection and remembrance as we honor the courage and sacrifice of our fallen veterans. Today, our freedom is ours because of the heroes who dedicated their lives to protecting it. We are forever in debt to those who paid the ultimate price for our liberty. Please join me in taking time to reflect today. Sincerely, Guy Ciarrocchi | | | ---|---|--- | | | | PAID FOR BY OUR GUY FOR CONGRESS Our Guy For Congress, P.O. Box 515, Glenmoore, PA

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Wishing you a Safe, Healthy, and Happy Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
---
| | Today, I’m remembering all of the brave fallen heroes in the United States Armed Forces from both New Jersey and across the country. We will never forget their unwavering commitment to our freedom, and we are all eternally grateful for their sacrifices and bravery.

One of the privileges of my job is getting to work with members of the armed service. As a member of the Homeland Security Committee, I see everyday how important their commitment is to our freedom, and that devotion is why I give back and work with the VA. My promise to them, and to you, is this: **I will work my hardest to protect and provide for the brave patriots who defend us.**

As we celebrate today, I ask you to keep in mind the people who have given their lives to protect our country home and abroad. No matter your circumstances, please remember to celebrate respectfully and safely and to take a moment to give thanks for our freedom – for which so many have sacrificed so much.

I wish you all a safe and happy Memorial Day.

Yours,

Josh
---
|
---

name: Hillsdale College via CIntel
subject: Is socialism on the rise?
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vWhnydThTN

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oWhnydThTN

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Dear Fellow American,

A few years ago, New York magazine posed the question: "When Did Everyone Become a Socialist?"

This is, of course, an exaggeration. But it makes clear that the far-Left in America aims to bring about a "Great Reset" of our economy and our way of life.

In a recent issue of Imprimis-Hillsdale College's digest of liberty-author Michael Rectenwald described the economic goal of the Great Reset as "'capitalism with Chinese characteristics'-a two-tiered economy, with profitable monopolies and
the state on top and socialism for the majority below."

Today, as a supporter of liberty, you can help Hillsdale College understand the extent of support for-and opposition to-socialism.

Your input will help us develop the most effective strategy to fight for the future of liberty in our country.

Will you please share your views on the political, social, and cultural forces promoting socialism in our country by completing your National Survey on Socialism using the secure link below?

https://lp.hillsdale.edu/take-the-national-survey-on-socialism/

Sadly, socialism is becoming increasingly popular, especially among the young. You may be aware of a SHOCKING survey showing that...

. . . during the COVID-19 pandemic, support for socialism among those 16 to 23 years old jumped to 49 percent, and socialism also increased in popularity among all ages.

Why is this?

Draconian government lockdown measures destroyed businesses and eliminated competitors of corporate monopolists. These policies were a dream come true for corporate socialists.

At the same time, too many young Americans have not been taught what socialism really means. Or the dismal history of its failures wherever it has been tried.

This is a result of the far-Left's "long march through the institutions"-including colleges and universities, K-12 schools, and the media.

Marxist-inspired critical race theory is a prime example. Once confined to the classrooms of far-Left professors, critical race theory has infiltrated every level of government, as well as school boards and even the military.

But it is in our schools where the effects of critical race theory-which teaches young Americans to categorize their fellow citizens according to race-are most dangerous.

You may recall that many young Americans with college degrees who championed socialist policies participated in the riots and violence we witnessed in the summer of 2020.

In the face of well-financed and widespread efforts to destroy free markets and individual liberty, Hillsdale's work to reach and teach millions has never been more urgently needed.

That's why I need your thoughts quickly.

By responding to the National Survey on Socialism in the next five days, your honest opinions will help us understand the recent rise of socialism and develop our strategy to reach and teach increasing millions of Americans on behalf of
liberty.

We hope to hear from friends representing every part of the country.

Education is the surest way to turn back this dangerous trend. And your partnership with Hillsdale College is critical.

As Winston Churchill said, "Socialism is the philosophy of failure, the creed of ignorance, and the gospel of envy."

If you agree, please represent your state and take the National Survey on Socialism today.

https://lp.hillsdale.edu/take-the-national-survey-on-socialism/

Despite the recent successes of the far-Left, we must remember-it is NEVER too late to defend liberty through education.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Gary Sinise
subject: Most don't know this about Memorial Day
body:
Peter,

It's Gary Sinise here.

You may know me from my roles in movies like
Apollo 13 and The Green Mile - but the role that changed my life,
and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans,
and first responders, was as Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump.

This year, I'm honored to again serve as an
Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in
Washington, D.C.

The National Memorial Day Parade is an event
like no other ... and this year, the American Veterans Center is
bringing it BACK in full force, with 5,000+ participants marching
down Constitution Avenue before a crowd of 250,000+ in-person, as
well as T.V. broadcast that reaches 100 million households around
the country.

Peter, the National Memorial Day Parade is
PRIMARILY FUNDED by private contributions, including from
generous Americans like you. Will you help chip in today to help
support the Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?

Support the National Memorial Day Parade

When the American Veterans Center first called
me more than 15 years ago and invited me to the National Memorial
Day Parade, I was honored to join the event to remember our
fallen heroes.

The American Veterans Center revived the Parade
in 2005 after it had been dormant for nearly 70 years. Now, in
2022, they're bringing the Parade back to its full-scale glory
after two years of pandemic precautions.

Every American should be proud that generations
of our brave servicemen and servicewomen, from the last of our
"Greatest Generation" heroes of World War II to our veterans from
Afghanistan and Iraq, will get the public thanks for their
service that they deserve this year.

But I know that the defenders and families of
the fallen who will join me in the National Memorial Day Parade
will be thinking of their friends and loved ones who made the
ultimate sacrifice for our country - the brave Americans we honor
on Memorial Day.

Sadly, our country is losing hundreds of World
War II veterans each day, so the mission of the National Memorial
Day Parade is more important now than ever - as this year's event
may be the LAST opportunity for our WWII heroes to commemorate
their friends who never came home.

Please make your most generous contribution of
$25, $50, $75, $150, $250, $500, to support the National Memorial
Day Parade and make it the tribute that our nation's heroes
deserve.

Support the National Memorial Day Parade

The National Memorial Day Parade costs a
fraction of other major public events to put on each year. In
fact, the entire parade costs about as much as just THREE
balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in
the Rose Bowl Parade!

That means that every dollar that's contributed
by a generous American like you makes a difference for this
important event.

Your support of the American Veterans Center
not only helps the Parade, but also supports great programs like:

Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans
from World War II to Today before we lose these stories forever.
Hold our National Youth Summit which allows
young students a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from
WWII, Korea, and Vietnam.
Produce veteran focused T.V. documentaries and
specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

Please join me in support of the National
Memorial Day Parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, $250,
$500, or more right now.

But no matter how much you can give to this
important cause, I want to ask something additional of you.On
Memorial Day - this year and every year - please join me in
honoring those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our freedoms.

Come to the National Memorial Day Parade in
D.C. Visit a local veterans' cemetery. Send a note to a family of
the fallen. Or simply take a quiet moment to think about the men
and women who gave their lives to defend OUR country.

I believe we can never do enough to express our
gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, but
we can always do a little more.

Peter, thank you for your support of
America's heroes, and please - if you're able, make a gift to
help fund the National Memorial Day Parade right now.

Sincerely,Gary SiniseHonorary Grand
MarshalNational Memorial Day Parade

Support the National Memorial Day Parade

 

The mission of the American Veterans Center and
the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote
the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty
service personnel from World War II through today.

A non-profit educational organization, the AVC
and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from
people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible.

© American Veterans Center1100 N. Glebe Road
Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: Mark, 2022 Membership Services
subject: I didn’t know you were a Democrat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| |
---
| | |
Fellow Conservative,

**I have bad news. You 've failed to renew your Republican Membership and have broken a 2-year support streak.**

I am so disappointed. You seemed like such a great Republican supporter. And now that Republicans are on the verge of winning back the House Majority, we need you more than ever.

**[Will you renew for 2022 and become a Sustaining Member of our Republican team one more time? name: Gwen Moore
subject: Remembering their sacrifice
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
body:
My Sisters and Brothers,

This Memorial Day, let us give our deepest gratitude to those courageous sons, daughters, husbands, wives, brothers, and sisters, who have given their lives in service to our country. These heroes made the ultimate sacrifice to protect the righteous ideals which unite us as one people, one nation.

Today, and every day, I give thanks to the brave souls who died to protect the nation so that you and I can keep walking towards a better future.

Because of our heroes’ sacrifices, I know that better is possible. For them, and our children, we march on. May those who have fallen rest in peace and may their memory always be a blessing.

In remembrance, #WeRepresent

Gwen
Proudly representing Wisconsin's Fourth Congressional District
Proud Co-Lead of the Violence Against Women Act
100% Pro-Choice

Stay Connected! Text "GWEN" to 313131 for campaign updates!
FB: https://www.facebook.com/GwenMooreforCongress
Twitter: https://twitter.com/Gwen4Congress
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/gwenmooreforcongress

Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
Gwen Moore for Congress
P.O. Box 16646
c/o Evans & Katz
Milwaukee, WI 53216
United States

name: =?utf-8?Q?Greg=20Lopez?=
subject: The Ultimate Sacrifice Is Not Forgotten
body:
As long as we have breath, we will carry the torch.

http://www.lopez2022.com?utm_source=Greg+Lopez+For+Governor&utm_campaign=70ded3cff7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_05_29_06_24&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_9fbc254cd8-70ded3cff7-584862049

Dear Coloradan,

Today is a blessed and hallowed day for all Americans. It is the day, we remember the bravest of our number, who gave all they had so that you and I can have all that we have. It is our honor and profound privilege to have men and women with such outstanding character and magnificent courage walking among the us in complete selfless service. We honor you today!

As governor of Colorado, I will never forget that my job is to protect and uphold the very values and freedoms from our Constitution that they were willing to die to defend. I count this as one of my greatest obligations and duties.

I challenge all of us to offer a moment of silence for these heroes and a prayer for a well-ordered society where liberty, justice, and peace remain the pillars that uphold us as a people for generations to come.

============================================================
** (http://www.lopez2022.com?utm_source=Greg+Lopez+For+Governor&utm_campaign=70ded3cff7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2022_05_29_06_24&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_9fbc254cd8-70ded3cff7-584862049)
We are Coloradans who want to freely and responsibly live our best lives. We refuse to be told by so-called experts and politicians how we must live. Together, we can flip the power back to the people by electing a new governor that’s one of us, for all of us. Vote for Greg Lopez, a proven servant leader who puts people over politics to make Colorado the State of Systemic Opportunity for all.

Democrat Nikki Budzinski is running for Congress in Illinois' 13th District. This district is currently in Republican hands, and is by far one of the most competitive in the entire country.

Nikki has spent her life fighting for workers and working families by strengthening unions nationwide, and she was instrumental in passing a $15 minimum wage in Illinois while working as a senior advisor for Governor JB Pritzker. Most recently, Nikki served in President Biden's administration in the office that implemented the American Rescue Plan.

Nikki believes in the American Dream and will bring people together to level the playing field and rebuild the middle class so families have a shot to get ahead.

name: Ann Roe
subject: My thoughts this Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANN ROE FOR CONGRESS Ann Roe for Congress
body:
|
---
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45903409/348010272/-766995749?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzU3ZDUzMy0yOWUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PyYb8c64Iz5h_2OGmUhycmgemKxYBUdWDNadxtsyW44=&emci=387943d9-d9dd-ec11-b656-281878b85110&emdi=bc57d533-29e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=15639879)

Hi team,

I want to take time today and offer up my thoughts this Memorial Day.

Today is a time for us to think of, commemorate, and honor the brave men and women of our armed forces **who made the ultimate sacrifice and gave their lives for our freedoms and our way of life as Americans.** And as the daughter and niece of men who fought for our country, **I will always honor and value the service that all service members give to our nation.**
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45903412/348010275/-766995749?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTY2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzU3ZDUzMy0yOWUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PyYb8c64Iz5h_2OGmUhycmgemKxYBUdWDNadxtsyW44=&emci=387943d9-d9dd-ec11-b656-281878b85110&emdi=bc57d533-29e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=15639879)
---

My father, Harry Spellman, served in the Korean War. His time in the service helped to shape who he was as a husband and father, and defined our family and our values. I have always been so proud to call him dad.

**When times are hardest, we see the very best of what it means to be an American. We see endurance and generosity and bravery – the values my father instilled in my brothers and me. We see people come together to lift one another up.**

Time and time again we've seen that this year in so many of our fellow Americans. **But we especially see those values in our service members who were willing to lay down their lives for all of us.**

We see and honor their sacrifice today, tomorrow, and every single day. Because of them, we are a stronger nation.

I hope you have a peaceful Memorial Day.

– Ann Roe

* * *
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Doug White
subject: Honoring fallen service members
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DOUG WHITE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| |
---
As many of us wrap up a holiday long weekend, I wanted to take a moment to truly honor the meaning of Memorial Day.

This is a day of remembrance for U.S. service members who sacrificed their lives for American democracy. We often honor their sacrifices with physical monuments and moments of silence – but we must also honor them with action.

Recently, we have seen just how fragile our democracy is – and just how far some extremists in our political sphere will go to overthrow it.

So this Memorial Day, part of how I am commemorating fallen soldiers, sailors, airmen, and other service members is by recommitting myself to defending our democratic values at all costs.

If they can give their lives for our country, I can take a stand against those who threaten our democracy and our freedom. I hope you will join me.

– Doug

| PAID FOR BY DOUG WHITE FOR CONGRESS
---
Doug White for Congress
PO Box 9674
Yakima, WA 98909
United States

Doug White is running to represent Washington’s 4th Congressional District in Congress..

Peter, I wanted to take a moment to explain why I’m running to be re-elected as your attorney general.

Growing up, I didn’t have power or privilege. I was born and raised to hardworking, loving parents in Brooklyn and received my education through the New York City public school system. **At an early age, I learned that families like my own were consistently being taken advantage of by New York’s powerful and corrupt. **
When I made history and became the first Black woman ever to serve as New York’s attorney general, I knew that I had to take this opportunity to fight for the voiceless and vulnerable – and that’s exactly what I’ve done. **But with Election Day only months away, I’m urgently calling on this grassroots community to help me make history once again so I can continue my hard work.**

[**I need to raise another $7,392 by tomorrow at midnight to hit my $15,000 end-of-month goal for May, so please: Will you donate $5 or more right now so I can continue fighting for New York?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45904582/348013291/379053316?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk486-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZTQwMjllNC0yOWUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5iuY7ttT1jueN_hxRNuqZgm9ufoxIDdRkvMaxWteN4I=)

| MAGA Republicans are obsessed with pushing our country back in time by stripping women of their right to make health care decisions.

This month, we’ve heard plans for a national ban on abortion, outlawing the Plan B pill, and making all contraception illegal unless you are married. I’m sure there’s more to come.

**And here in Kansas, abortion is on the primary ballot this August — voters will soon decide on an amendment to remove abortion rights from the Kansas Constitution.**

_Look, here’s the truth: the right for women to make decisions about their own bodies is a fundamental human right, and it’s time for us to fight with everything we’ve got to protect it._

[I am running for U.S. Senate to protect reproductive freedom for every American from far-right extremists. **Meet Mark:** Reverend Mark Holland is a fourth generation Kansan and a third generation United Methodist Pastor. He has dedicated his life to the service of others, as a father, pastor, community leader, elected official, and non-profit leader. Mark is the co-founder and executive director of Mainstream UMC, a global advocacy organization dedicated to greater inclusion in the church. He has served the Church in Kansas for over 25 years, most recently as the Senior Pastor of Trinity Community Church in Kansas City, Kansas for 19 years.

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45906627/348017194/1893886755?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWM4MGU0OC0yYmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dHYwNLog0WqYFcMFq9KVSKRrlmj9i9HEQbdgg_2ziNM=&emci=b48d7e11-82df-ec11-b656-281878b85110&emdi=71c80e48-2be0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=11913588) [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45906635/348017196/1893886755?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MWM4MGU0OC0yYmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dHYwNLog0WqYFcMFq9KVSKRrlmj9i9HEQbdgg_2ziNM=&emci=b48d7e11-82df-ec11-b656-281878b85110&emdi=71c80e48-2be0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=11913588) Today, I wanted to take a moment of reflection as we honor and remember those who gave their lives for this country.

We can never fully repay those who made the ultimate sacrifice while defending safety, freedoms, and values. So we must honor their memories by keeping our promises to today’s service members, veterans, and their families. I'll continue to do everything in my power to improve healthcare and benefits for all who serve and who have served, I'll make sure they have the support they need for any future job or education opportunities, and I'll continue to advocate for our Gold Star families.

To all, I wish you a safe and relaxing Memorial Day.

–Lisa Blunt Rochester ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress

Peter, Happy Memorial Day! Demand for Team Baxter yard signs is through the roof, as the costs for campaign materials continue to skyrocket... Thanks, Joe Biden.
My Campaign Manager Dercy informed me this morning that we need to raise $1,791 by midnight tomorrow night in order to purchase more yard signs for voters in the District. Peter, you have been a part of our movement for limited government and individual liberty since Day One. I'm coming to you today because I need your help. [Make a donation of $20.22 or more to help me raise $1,791 by 11:59 pm tomorrow night >>>](https://email.baxterforcongress.com/e3t/Ctc/OR+113/d2p7Ng04/VVwYlL4ywP9xW6Y2rrw8kFn-gW6fpr4J4KJs4lMrH-SB3lLBmV1-WJV7CgQ_9W6BJ62l6J-0F7W141sDY2QQJqvN6n1bDJPH34lW8Kv8ZQ85Fl0lW2f2ZWF97hyPjW7rCzRw6XbXJkW3--Q3228XqkjW3ZQ0Qq7wDpsWW1-rccq7fdKSDN893sSx7QQwdVyfxyH2PLX28W5w4r8x1slBkXW2GDFt47Zz82rW2g1HBT11Pb39W6hzfFd2FYNy6W3SFnst8jmr7ZW4wsT928PH7F0W8yJ8QQ4mQMTCW2wnT8629f4xmW1YcGkP7spYypW8FyTWC3MwSMrW2dXM7X11nbFf2CH1)

name: Rushern Baker
subject: Memorial Day
body:
http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2 [http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2]

Friend,
Coming from a military family, Memorial Day has special meaning to me. My dad served in the 82 nd Airborne Division during the Vietnam War. He was badly wounded and later awarded a Purple Heart and Bronze Star. He was one of the lucky ones. He made it back alive, when so many others did not.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93538/images/Millitary_5.jpeg]
On this day we honor our servicemen and women who gave the ultimate sacrifice protecting our Democracy. The thought of losing a loved one at war is almost unimaginable, and yet it is inevitable in war. We owe our deepest gratitude to all our fallen heroes.
May you all enjoy a blessed Memorial Day.
Thank you,
http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2 [http://secure.ngpvan.com/t_OakHAu1Eyexxm3ZVTwZw2]

name: Jared Moskowitz
subject: My reflection on Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jared Moskowitz for Congress
body:
Dear Friends,

Today, I’ll be spending time with my family as we barbecue and the kids enjoy an extra day off of school, but that’s not what Memorial Day is about. It’s easy to take today for granted, but our ability to
recognize this holiday is due to the great sacrifice of those who served and their families.

The men and women who have served our country inspire me to work towards a safer, more just, and more equitable future.

My family escaped the Holocaust, and today, I’m thinking of those who gave their lives fighting the Nazis and other tyrants throughout history. They made the ultimate sacrifice to fight evil, hate, and violence.

And as we witness attacks on democracy at home, in Ukraine, and across the globe, I’m thinking of all those who have defended freedom and democracy across generations.

During President Biden’s 2020 campaign, he said we were in a battle for the soul of the nation. That’s still true today, with politicians refusing to act in the face of attacks on our democracy, mass shootings, and restrictions on our rights.

On Memorial Day, I hope we can all be inspired by those who gave their lives fighting for our values as we all do our part to build a more perfect union.

With Gratitude,

Jared Moskowitz

Paid for by Jared Moskowitz for Congress
Jared Moskowitz for Congress
PO Box 8784
Coral Springs, FL 33065
United States

name: Liz Mathis
subject: We honor their sacrifices
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Liz Mathis for Congress
body:
Dear Peter, This Memorial Day, I’m thinking of all the honorable men and women in Iowa who have lost their lives in service to our country and its core principles. Throughout the generations, American service members – like both of my own parents, who served in World War II – have fought for our safety and to protect our Democracy. But words can never convey our gratitude to those who made the ultimate sacrifice with their lives. To all those who have lost family members, friends and loved ones: I assure you their service, sacrifice, and memory are not forgotten. **This Memorial Day, let us honor those sacrifices.** And every day, let us fight for what’s right in their memory. Liz

name: =?utf-8?Q?Ed=20Thelander=20for=20Congress?=
subject: Memorial Day, 2022
body:
Americans have observed Memorial Day for over 150 years because part of the rent for our democracy is to remember that freedom isn’t free. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/thelanderforcongress/meet-greet-in-waldoboro-8885330?e=f8a0a76a2b)
---
[](https://thelanderforcongress.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=035bf0620f01206429344ca39&id=0efcecc572&e=f8a0a76a2b)
Americans have observed Memorial Day for over 150 years because part of the rent for our democracy is to remember that freedom isn’t free. It’s not just a day for barbecues and welcoming summer, but also a time to honor the fallen. For those of us who served our country in harm’s way, Memorial Day conjures in our hearts and minds the brothers we lost. Today I think of two. Chief Petty Officer Adam Brown gave what Lincoln called his “last full measure of devotion” to America in the Komar Province of Afghanistan in 2010. Even though, and maybe even because, his right eye was failing, he aced the sniper course I taught with outstanding merit. He died in the field so others could live. Another face I remember today belongs to a 20-year veteran of the SEAL teams named Dave, who put his kids on the school-bus one day, kissed his wife on her way out the door, mowed their lawn, and then took his own life. Warriors tend to keep our feelings close-hold, even when reaching out to someone else is the best thing to do. I also consider these to be combat deaths, and there are far too many of them: more today than at any time in our country’s history. This is why we remember and honor the fallen by keeping their memories alive, and expressing our gratitude to the families whose loved ones accepted the ultimate sacrifice for us all. They would want this day to be one on which the sun would shine, and their families would be safe under the blessings of liberty.

clean_body: Americans have observed Memorial Day for over 150 years because part of the rent for our democracy is to remember that freedom isn’t free. | | | Americans have observed Memorial Day for over 150 years because part of the rent for our democracy is to remember that freedom isn’t free. It’s not just a day for barbecues and welcoming summer, but also a time to honor the fallen. For those of us who served our country in harm’s way, Memorial Day conjures in our hearts and minds the brothers we lost. Today I think of two. Chief Petty Officer Adam Brown gave what Lincoln called his “last full measure of devotion” to America in the Komar Province of Afghanistan in 2010. Even though, and maybe even because, his right eye was failing, he aced the sniper course I taught with outstanding merit. He died in the field so others could live. Another face I remember today belongs to a 20-year veteran of the SEAL teams named Dave, who put his kids on the school-bus one day, kissed his wife on her way out the door, mowed their lawn, and then took his own life. Warriors tend to keep our feelings close-hold, even when reaching out to someone else is the best thing to do. I also consider these to be combat deaths, and there are far too many of them: more today than at any time in our country’s history. This is why we remember and honor the fallen by keeping their memories alive, and expressing our gratitude to the families whose loved ones accepted the ultimate sacrifice for us all. They would want this day to be one on which the sun would shine, and their families would be safe under the blessings of liberty. | | | | | | | mailto:info@thelanderforcongress.com| | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | | | Copyright (C) 2022 Committee to Elect Ed Thelander for Congress, Inc.. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
We reflect and honor all those who gave the ultimate sacrifice to serve and protect our country.

| I’m so honored to represent San Diego, home to the largest population of military personnel in the country. As a proud military community, our community deeply understands the value of service and the immense sacrifice of our military members and their families. And for many in our community, Memorial Day brings up feelings of loss and grief.

This Memorial Day, as we reflect and honor all those who gave the ultimate sacrifice to serve and protect our country, let's also recommit ourselves to supporting service members and their families in every way we can.

With gratitude,

Sara

name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe220529?refcode=lpfe220529 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe220529?refcode=lpfe220529]
.
This Memorial Day, I join people across the country in remembering and honoring the service members who gave their lives for our country.
We can honor their memories by carrying on their fight for our country, our democracy, and our values. We do that by continuing to work together, by making our voices heard at the ballot box, and by standing up for our freedoms — freedoms they fought and died for.
We must work together to fight for our values — today and every day. Thank you for all you do to honor their sacrifice by working to build a more perfect union.
Together, we can do anything,

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Pausing to remember
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Every Memorial Day, we take the time to honor our brave U.S. service members who made the ultimate sacrifice in order to protect the freedoms we enjoy as Americans.

Those who put on the uniform to fight are some of the best among us. We owe them endless gratitude.

This Memorial Day, I’m recommitting to ensuring that our men and women in uniform returning from the battlefield are taken care of by the country they served. That means fighting for their healthcare, adequate housing, and mental health services. I’ll never stop speaking up for our heroes.

Please join me in honoring those who gave their lives for our country.

Happy Memorial Day,

Mike Thompson

name: Dr. Yadira Caraveo
subject: Holding space on Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914162/348024479/-2134616058?refcode=fl_em_na_20220530MemorialDay_dyc_allop_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYzhkNjYyMy0zMmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pdPYH8LNltQ0wEgwjDmE2b4cUH47nzqdD30r5R6jG4w=)
---
Peter, Today, on this Memorial Day, we honor those who made the ultimate sacrifice and lost their lives in service to our country. We also hold space for all of our veterans, who often return wounded on the inside and out. Too often, our brave servicemembers endure long-term health impacts, including PTSD and other mental health issues, from their time in combat. We as a country must make sure we live up to the promises we make to them — and ensure that no one who has served gets lost or left behind when they return to civilian life. To the veterans, thank you for your service. And thank you to the families who support our veterans and grieve their losses. May you find comfort in their sacrifice and bravery. Dr. Caraveo
---
| Yadira Caraveo is a pediatrician, state representative, and daughter of immigrants. Her parents raised her and her siblings on her father’s construction worker’s salary in Adams County. In the legislature, Dr. Caraveo has delivered for working Coloradans by standing up to the biggest corporate bullies and entrenched special interests.

Now, Dr. Caraveo is running for Congress to stand up for hard-working families she sees falling through the cracks. Dr. Caraveo would be the first Latina to represent Colorado in Congress and the only physician in Colorado’s congressional delegation. name: Emilia Sykes
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emilia Sykes for Congress
body:
Peter, I want to wish you and your family a peaceful and reflective Memorial Day. Today, I am thinking about those who have given everything in service to our country.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914684/348026845/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2QyZDVlNy0zMmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sWmauojAr5NIaQpP3vkUHIDKVjSkKGG-hKDfI3c7Vb8=&emci=f7329340-0edd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=e3d2d5e7-32e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=11703046)
Peter,

I want to wish you and your family a peaceful and reflective Memorial Day. Today, I am thinking about those who have given everything in service to our country. Millions of servicemen and servicewomen have paid the ultimate price in order to preserve our values and freedoms for future generations. We owe them and their families a debt of gratitude. **I hope you’ll join me today in a moment of reflection to honor the sacrifices made by all who have served, and are currently serving.** _Thank you. _ On days like today, I also recognize the need to reaffirm our support for these heroes by ensuring they have the resources they and their families have the resources they need to adjust back to civilian life. We cannot waver in our commitment to those who have answered the call, and must ensure that we are all working to build a future that lives up to the values and freedoms they work so earnestly to protect. With gratitude, _Emilia _

---

| Emilia Sykes is running to represent Ohio's 13th Congressional District. To learn more, please visit [EmiliaSykesForCongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914685/348026852/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2QyZDVlNy0zMmUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sWmauojAr5NIaQpP3vkUHIDKVjSkKGG-hKDfI3c7Vb8=&emci=f7329340-0edd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=e3d2d5e7-32e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=11703046)

Campaigns cost money, and we’ve set big goals to reach as many folks as possible to build our movement. = | = = = Whatever you can give goes directly to keeping our campaign on track. That money goes to hiring staff, buying the technology we need to reach voters, powering our Convention operation, and keeping us on the road to continue meeting folks in their own communities. That’s why this deadline is so important, friend. Our campaign isn’t powered by insiders or flashy donors, but if everyone is able to chip in, we can meet this deadline and continue growing this people-powered movement. name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Recognizing those who made the ultimate sacrifice.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer |
body:
Peter,

Memorial Day isn't just a day off from school or work – it's a day dedicated to remembering and honoring those who gave their lives fighting for our country.

Today, our nation takes a moment to honor the patriotic Americans who gave the ultimate sacrifice in the line of duty. Because of their sacrifice and commitment, my grandma was able to survive World War II, immigrate to the U.S., and become the proudest American citizen you could’ve ever met. Because of that sacrifice and commitment, my family and I have the privilege of living in a world where we are granted the freedoms we share as Americans.

Showing gratitude for those who are no longer with us is important. But I think honoring those we've lost should extend beyond just today. In addition to honoring their sacrifices, we also need to show an appreciation for the freedoms they died for.

This Memorial Day, we should all be conscious of the fact that military action often comes at a significant price. That means America needs a smart, strategic approach to foreign policy. It means that when military action occurs, our nation needs to provide the training and equipment our service members need. It means we must do everything we can to bring each service member home safely.

I hope you’ll join me in commemorating the brave Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice. Today, we honor the fallen and rededicate ourselves to protecting those who currently serve and the veterans who are still here, making us proud every single day.

It's an honor to represent you,

Derek Kilmer

---------------------------------------------
| Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer |
---------------------------------------------

PO Box 1381
Tacoma WA 98402 United States

name: Tom =?utf-8?b?T+KAmUhhbGxlcmFu?=
subject: This Memorial Day, we honor the service members who made the ultimate sacrifice.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914961/348028100/-1425992037?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2IwN2I1ZC0zNGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Dw1BoRWbLmE6evA72ShebM_GX-cZ09y8AxPLAWu7M0s=&emci=54e3dbfb-12dd-ec11-b656-281878b8c32f&emdi=27b07b5d-34e0-ec11-b656-281878b85110&ceid=18634996) Friend,

Memorial Day is a time to remember those who have sacrificed their lives serving our great nation in military uniform.

It’s a day to honor those who gave their lives to pursue liberty and the very idea of the United States. We owe them a tremendous debt of gratitude. The highest price of freedom is a cost paid by a brave few. I am forever grateful for their sacrifice.

We must honor their bravery and recommit to the ideals for which they fought. Today I am keeping the families of those who lost loved ones on the battlefield in my thoughts, and I pray for those still in harm’s way today.

Let’s recognize their service and sacrifice for the freedoms that we all enjoy today by lighting a candle in their memory.

Never forget,

\-- Tom

clean_body: Friend, Memorial Day is a time to remember those who have sacrificed their lives serving our great nation in military uniform. It’s a day to honor those who gave their lives to pursue liberty and the very idea of the United States. We owe them a tremendous debt of gratitude. The highest price of freedom is a cost paid by a brave few. I am forever grateful for their sacrifice. We must honor their bravery and recommit to the ideals for which they fought. Today I am keeping the families of those who lost loved ones on the battlefield in my thoughts, and I pray for those still in harm’s way today. Let’s recognize their service and sacrifice for the freedoms that we all enjoy today by lighting a candle in their memory. Never forget, \-- Tom | To learn more, please visit | Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress Tom O'Halleran PO Box 63992 Phoenix, AZ 85082 United States

body:
and we’re still falling short! |

| | Hi there, it’s Charlie Crist’s finance team.

By now, you’ve probably heard that we set an aggressive fundraising goal of raising $70,000 by May 31. We’re reaching out to let you know that while we’ve made significant progress toward this goal, we’re not there yet.

**_[To close the gap, we’re asking everyone to pitch in a few dollars by tomorrow at midnight. Can you do that now?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914959/348028096/1828376163?amount=25&refcode=20220530_f_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNWIwN2I1ZC0zNGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W_kHN0zaDhNu0xiRdueVhgVo4RBscyUBpKkeS1fvBFE=)_ If we fail to reach this goal, our chances of defeating Gov. Ron DeSantis will plummet.**

Unlike Gov. DeSantis, Charlie doesn’t rely on right-wing, billionaire donors and their deep pockets. He depends on his incredible grassroots team, and that’s why we're coming to you today.

This is our first major monthly deadline since the initial draft decision on _Roe v. Wade_ was released. That means we need to show everyone — including DeSantis — that we have what it takes to defeat his extremist agenda and protect reproductive freedom.

We wouldn’t ask if we didn’t need your help, friend. **If you can spare any amount, we know Charlie would be very grateful._[Please contribute by tomorrow at midnight!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45914960/348028097/1828376163?amount=25&refcode=20220530_f_2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNWIwN2I1ZC0zNGUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W_kHN0zaDhNu0xiRdueVhgVo4RBscyUBpKkeS1fvBFE=)_**

We profoundly appreciate your support!

— Finance Department, Team Crist

name: =?utf-8?Q?Karina=20Lipsman?=
subject: Karina Lipsman Reflects on Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KARINA FOR CONGRESS
body:
As your Congresswoman I will never let America enter an ill-advised war where our brave men and women are used as global police. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/8d8ab7a17789/karina-for-congress-9025721?e=22db41c18e)
---
PeterWhile millions of Americans celebrate a three-day weekend, it is imperative that each and every one of us take the time to remember the true meaning behind Memorial Day.Today, we should all honor the sacrifices made by over 500,000 brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice for our Country. Without the brave heroism of each soldier, the freedoms, and liberties we enjoy would not be possible.I encourage everyone in Virginia's 8th Congressional District, as well as all Americans nationwide, to remember our fallen heroes today and every day.
[](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=190431878d&e=22db41c18e)
| [VISIT MY WEBSITE TO LEARN MORE](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=fcc172e327&e=22db41c18e)
---
I visited Arlington National Cemetery in Virginia’s 8th District to pay my respects to many of our nation's greatest heroes.As your Congresswoman I will never let America enter an ill-advised war where our brave men and women are used as global police. I will also oppose sending blank checks to foreign countries and I will back legislation that ensures we have transparency over how foreign aid is being used. I will never send our brave soldiers off to fight unnecessary wars where the U.S. is not being directly threatened.May God bless you and continue to bless the families of our fallen heroes.Sincerely,Karina
|
---
| | | | [](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=eb0a2250b2&e=22db41c18e)| [](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=4736924b97&e=22db41c18e)| [](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=4b0fe7e9f8&e=22db41c18e)| [](https://karinaforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5169078bc3370360935103e65&id=bb77c4dffa&e=22db41c18e)

Heck, you 've probably seen it with your own two eyes, in prices at the supermarket, your child's school curriculum, and at the gas pump. | I need the conservative movement to activate more than ever this year. Our country has never been in such a position as this, and it's time to show these socialist sycophants who's boss - the U.S. of A. name: Sheriff Joe Lombardo & Team
subject: We remember the fallen.
body:
On behalf of myself and our entire team, I hope you’re able to join us on this Memorial Day in remembering our fallen heroes.
---
| | | | | | |
---
|
---
| Peter,
On behalf of myself and our entire team, I hope you're able to join us on this Memorial Day in remembering our fallen heroes.
We must never, ever forget their noble sacrifice. These brave men and women gave their lives for the United States of America, and we are only the Land of the Free BECAUSE of the Brave.
Let's keep that in mind as we remember our fallen service members, and say a prayer for them and their families.
---
| [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001CS74_8z9Y58zzKt9lm-uUafyJ5yRXbDNqzQd6LR8GAOiheESbZB6LviXsgq2nOXjY70HOoq5K2Zv1C-0A140LRkJm8N34o8qC2JxcrUHQ6pYMnwzHRA0WsEIEFoxKHh-mXoRhajOCwNonV0386-rnyA-V_jEv_w_&c=5qHzCRs1wsPIWPIpNr7wSnMUXmH3UPJWmt3mLljIJqthDMG5_diaWg==&ch=CGZzIbwEwK7JpHQ2Dm8bR8AIm59HQ5CQNi20Yo5_Oxy9Jo4dGIzWKw==)
---
| Putting one's life on the line for their country and their communities is as honorable of a sacrifice as there is. So, please join me in paying your respects to these heroes. We must ALWAYS remember them - especially today on Memorial Day - and ensure that those sacrifices they made were not made in vain.
Thank you for honoring those who gave their lives for the unmatched rights and freedoms we enjoy here in Nevada and all across the United States of America.
In Commemoration, Sheriff Joe Lombardo & Team
---
|
---

name: Wade Herring
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wade Herring for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/herringrunoff?refcode=whem0530 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/herringrunoff?refcode=whem0530]
Peter,
Memorial Day is a day of reflection. It is an opportunity for us to remember those we have lost and honor their sacrifice.
For those of us that live on, it is our duty to not only pay homage to this sacrifice but to remain mindful of the cost of freedom and liberty.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/91050/images/D371064E-1612-42FC-A675-8AC48A40A290.jpeg]
I hope you take this day to reflect on the meaning of this holiday, and to spend it with friends and family in safety and good health.
Thank you,
Wade Herring

| Consistent with federal law, House Majority PAC makes its federal contributions from one account (the “Contribution Account”) and engages in the rest of its political activity from the other account (the “Non-Contribution Account”). You may contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year to House Majority PAC's Contribution Account. You may contribute an unlimited amount to House Majority PAC's Non-Contribution Account. By donating through this email, you agree that the first $5,000 of your contribution will be deposited in House Majority PAC's Contribution Account and the remainder in House Majority PAC's Non-Contribution Account. **HUGE news out of Iowa, Peter – and it's not looking good. **
**FIRST:** After polling revealed more than two-thirds of likely Iowa voters don't want to reelect Sen. Chuck Grassley (R), Democrat Abby Finkenauer launched her campaign – giving Democrats the best chance in _decades_ to flip this Senate seat blue.
**BUT THEN:** Last month alone, Senate Republicans' campaign arm hauled in $8 MILLION to protect vulnerable incumbents like Sen. Grassley. It's a huge advantage they can use to dismantle Democrats' Senate majority.
**NOW:** **Abby is falling short of the $75,000 she needs to raise before her biggest end-of-month deadline TOMORROW. We are counting on your donation so we don't fall short.** With Senate control split 50-50, every single seat is indispensable when it comes to protecting our Democratic majority. We are counting on grassroots supporters like you to help us fight back against GOP spending and help Abby win!

name: Sen. Bryce Reeves
subject: Honoring the Fallen
disclaimer_text: Paid For by Reeves for Congress
body:
On Memorial Day
| | |
---
| | [](https://j0l1y7h.r.us-east-1.awstrack.me/L0/https:%2F%2Fbrycereeves.com/1/0100018114f84a5d-8b00d5d3-a2fe-4aaa-ac37-be30cd46f300-000000/X9OOmFU1dIbJydb7RTRqMgJKyic=271)
---
|
---
| Honoring the Fallen
---
|
---
| Friend,Today is Memorial Day, and we are called to honor and remember those who gave the ultimate sacrifice to protect our country and our way of life. In risking, and ultimately giving, their lives they continue to breath life into the greatest country the world has ever seen. While on Memorial Day we are asked to think of the brave men and women who died wearing the cloth of our country, it is every single day that we reap the rewards of their sacrifice. And though we can never thank them enough, I am asking you to join me in a prayer for our fallen and to take a moment to appreciate the gifts they have bestowed upon us.

Humbly,
---
| | |
---
| Sen. Bryce ReevesCaptainU.S. Army 1989-2002

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: Eternally Grateful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
God Bless the Americans who gave it all 🇺🇸 | |
---
| | | Friend,

_**Freedom doesn't come free.**_

Today and every day, we honor the men and women in uniform who went above and beyond the call of duty to defend our country, our freedoms, and our families. We are eternally grateful.

I also want to express my deep and profound gratitude to our Gold Star Families for their continued sacrifice and want to thank them for carrying on the legacies of our fallen servicemen and women.

_"Looking across this field, we see the scale of heroism and sacrifice. All who are buried here understood their duty. All stood to protect America. And all carried with them memories of a family that they hoped to keep safe by their sacrifice."_ — George W. Bush

**Have a safe Memorial Day and God Bless the United States Armed Forces.**

Best wishes,

Jake
---
|
---

name: Coach Tuberville
subject: 🇺🇸 Memorial Day 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tuberville for Senate, Inc
body:
They made the ultimate sacrifice for ALL of us.
| | | Peter, Our nation’s independence and freedom were won at a high cost. Ever since, generation after generation of patriots have answered the call to defend our liberty and way of life. America is the home of the free, thanks to the brave. As we celebrate Memorial Day 2022, let us remember and honor all of our courageous servicemen and servicewomen who’ve made the ultimate sacrifice for ALL of us. We owe those who’ve lost their lives in the line of duty to our nation a debt of gratitude that can never be fully repaid. Yet we must always make every effort to do so, by fighting to preserve and protect all they laid their lives on the line to defend.
---
---
id:2022-05-20-18:21:14:174t
---
Today, let us pray for our fallen soldiers, and reflect on how they’ve shaped our great nation and the world we live in today. It is because of them that our flag still waves as a beacon of light and hope. God bless our fallen and God bless America. \- Coach
---
id:2022-05-26-18:47:30:887t
---

Hello,
I hate to email you like this, but this is urgent.
We set a goal to raise another $250,000 before our critical end-of-month deadline to keep our ads up and fully fund our campaign to defeat Chuck Grassley. **But with less than 48 hours to go, I just spoke with my team, and they’ve informed me that we are on track to fall short.**
Honestly, this is a serious setback. In a race this close, every dollar is going to matter going up against Chuck Grassley and Mitch McConnell’s Super PAC millions. But we can’t give up.
My team is going to have an emergency Zoom meeting to figure out what we do next, but I already know this: **I need grassroots donors like you to chip in whatever you can afford now if we’re going to defeat Chuck Graslsey, turn Iowa blue, and protect our Democratic Senate majority.**
We can’t afford to fall short of our goal now, but I don’t take money from corporate PACs. Instead, I’m counting on you now more than ever. Please will you give now?
Here’s the best link to make a donation now: [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mikefranken_email**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45915828/348032589/-42398318?refcode=em20220530mid-nd&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C35%2C50%2C100%2C250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDdlMmQ1OS0zYWUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WJ9Fjre6-Wo-i18nunpp0rF6j5pT9qaQamW7Cegn5IY=)
Anything you can chip in now would make a huge difference. $5, $10, or whatever you can afford goes a long way toward defeating Grassley.
Thank you for being a part of this team,
Mike Franken, U.S. Navy (ret.)
