clean_body: The New York Times is out with another hit piece that focuses on my skin color instead of the issues important in this Senate race. | Peter, The race-based attacks against me just keep coming. As much as we try to focus this campaign on the issues that matter to voters like gas prices, record-high inflation, and crime, the Democrats and their allies in the media only want to talk about race. Check out this recent headline from The New York Times : The liberal "journalists" at the Times just don't understand Black Republicans, so they build their stories about our Senate race around quotes like this one: | "Mr. Walker was merely a vessel for the G.O.P. and Mr. Trump's ambitions." And this: "He's a puppet on a string, and somebody's pulling those strings really good." That's absolutely ridiculous. Let me be clear about something: I'm Herschel Walker, and I make my own decisions. Nobody tells me what to do. I'm running for the Senate because Raphael Warnock is taking our country in the wrong direction and does not represent Georgia values. I'm running to unite Americans and get our nation back on track before it's too late. It's becoming clearer by the day the Democrats and the liberal media are going to do everything in their power to distract voters from real issues and the disastrous Biden agenda. After all, if this race is a referendum on our unstable economy, record-high prices of groceries, unsecure border, or catastrophic pullout of troops from Afghanistan, Democrats will lose...and they know it. Your donation will help us ensure that voters understand who I am and what I stand for. If we're going to win this race, IT'S CRITICAL WE GO ON OFFENSE AND FOCUS THIS RACE ON ISSUES - NOT DISTRACTIONS. Please me stand up to these attacks. I am honored to have you in my corner. Thank you, Herschel Walker Republican Nominee for Senate | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

name: Laurie Teper
subject: A message from Courtney Geels - Volunteers Needed!
body:
Hey Volunteers!

I have put information about this out on the general Courtney Geels for Congress emails that go out weekly. But we need help on June 7th in multiple ways!!

I have been volunteering at the Boys and Girls Club of Durham weekly since the beginning of the year, and I've been able to build great relationships with the staff and some of the kids. The 13-year-old I work with a lot for tutoring congratulated me the Wednesday after the primary when I was there (he was watching for my name on TV)! That was really sweet.

I'm trying to fundraise FOR the Boys and Girls Club for this event. I will be buying pizzas for us to pass out to the kids. The club runs on donations, and the kids only have to pay $10/year to go to the club. It is mostly minority children, most of them from single parent or foster homes. The club pours a lot of energy into each individual child, keeping up with grades and behavioral needs. They have had great success, even into the teenage years. It costs a lot of money just for utilities for the building, and I have noticed they don't have many snacks and food for the kids when they have early release days or days off where they are at the club all day long.

What I need from you (one or both):
1\. Donate! You can write a check and mail it to my PO box or bring it the day of, June 7th if you would like to volunteer (Checks to be written to: "Boys & Girls clubs of Durham and Orange counties". If you want to mail it, you can mail it to me before next week to Courtney Geels, PO Box 995, Hillsborough, NC 27278). This contribution would be tax-deductible.
2\. Volunteer! We would love to have a big group with Courtney Geels for Congress T-Shirts on at 4pm to distribute pizzas and meet the kids and staff. It would be great to SHOW this area of our district that Republicans love them and want to help see them thrive. If you would like to volunteer for this event, please respond to this email.

The location of the Boys and Girls club is: 1010 Martin Luther King Jr. Pkwy Suite 300, Durham, NC 27713.

Thanks y'all!!
Courtney

Laurie Teper
Volunteer Coordinator
Courtney Geels for Congress NC04
Email: [volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com](mailto:volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com)
Cell: (847)212-5651
[courtneygeelsforcongress.com](http://courtneygeelsforcongress.com)

Hello,
My team just wrapped up an emergency fundraising meeting. Right now, Chuck Grassley is smearing me across the entire state with a new TV attack ad, and Mitch McConnell is raising millions more to stop me from turning Iowa blue.
But with less than 36 hours until my most important end-of-month deadline of the entire campaign, we just checked ActBlue – and our grassroots fundraising has slowed way down. **If I’m going to send Chuck Grassley packing and protect our Democratic Senate majority, I’m counting on a surge of donations from people like you now.**
The polling shows Grassley has never been more vulnerable, and we’re gaining ground. But Iowa was one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2020, and it’ll be even closer this year. If we miss our $250,000 fundraising goal now, we will not be able to reach all of Iowa with our counter ads and compete with Mitch’s millions.
**I’m not giving up – but I’m depending on you to maintain our momentum and win. If we turn Iowa blue this November, Democrats are guaranteed to protect our majority in the Senate. So I have to ask:**[**Please, will you chip in $5 or more right now – every dollar you can afford before my critical end-of-month deadline – to help me defeat Chuck Grassley, turn Iowa blue, and expand our Democratic Senate majority?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/45922978/348079899/-42398318?refcode=em20220530pm2-nd&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C35%2C50%2C100%2C250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDI4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZDQ5NDMzZS03YWUwLWVjMTEtYjY1Ni0yODE4NzhiODUxMTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WTycwJmw3rTHjwitV7iWZ98cPEoQEqQB8xBqCdKU1kc=)

This campaign is a grassroots movement —every donation, both big and small — is critical to our campaign! Our May deadline is approaching, and we are close to our goal! We need to just add 50 donors before midnight TOMORROW! Will you be one of the 50 and help us get to our goal?? Please, chip in today and help us hit our mid-month goal! Friend, I need your help.

Our massive FEC deadline is TOMORROW and we haven't raised the money we need to defend the Senate.

McConnell's megadonor machine has already begun hitting Democrats with attack ads -- and I don't want to think about the havoc they'll wreak if they outraise us again.

**One seat is all Republicans need to grind our progress to a _screeching_ halt.**

We simply _CANNOT_ let them outraise us, not with President Biden's bold agenda hanging in the balance. So I'll ask you again:

**[Will you rush in $5 or more while you can still have a huge impact to make sure we defend our Senate majority?](https://go.dscc.org/go/7283?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20220530-Chuck-SchumerLastCall-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20220530-Chuck-SchumerLastCall-Pros-sl1&akid=4501%2E9716520%2EtO8FzD)**

body:
Exclusive - Trump Majority Membership
Exclusive - Trump Majority Membership

|
---

Friend,

_**We wanted to reach out to you with a special offer! You have been hand-selected for the Trump Majority Membership and helping America First Republicans defeat Democrats in 2022.**_

body:
The New York Times is out with another hit piece that focuses on my skin color instead of the issues important in this Senate race. __ |
[](http://links.stoppingsocialistdems.com/u/click?_t=4dcc2aab65fd4d0da6a107b0eef86d43&_m=0f3f1c5f5ca64ce9badeb6b5d542c484&_e=HLWI8Zb-MCDf94XpZzr4RETOIayYquukzz-ktj-vMt-CwIW5gSRV6HeUNzTj7yz9of363isWT45lUQoQaI79CGJO48xS5bU6lNO7Co_pOCh0gX3TteLGg-apHTIP382pOZmpecHu8eCF__s2NA9nkil5n3CWBxQcNgWZWTCbJ05U_VkEdc_Z0mBXD0BNPBg_Ovk_EmUjdS1iCBerOj7nRH0GbeTXdEwaMLbE-j5NDHBTPiyp5ImU5xUmvfdoSI2O-VODlI95HkMOReQXb4fOY52wocQ8DrfBPzoVcViweEvENCIY0TRQyqbpp5pKhstGPHJnE8RQ7miko1fng97VedCARCJtbogHUY0woDGPJDaECZca2Fmn7OrMoB3nzQm6mpZpaGbN2sHfQToFWX94mFolrU1Xk6YVWQqPbtKieYH4kR3qIeL8ZahaYNOFHZHxL4oSI5GhqKRl0Xe_s3EsOYeOmfB8iN8yhV9Wad0FbwohiNj1nOdZyBA0ntR1cCCj)

---
Peter,

The race-based attacks against me just keep coming.

As much as we try to focus this campaign on the issues that matter to voters like gas prices, record-high inflation, and crime, the Democrats and their allies in the media only want to talk about race.

Check out this recent headline from _The New York Times_ :

[](http://links.stoppingsocialistdems.com/u/click?_t=4dcc2aab65fd4d0da6a107b0eef86d43&_m=0f3f1c5f5ca64ce9badeb6b5d542c484&_e=HLWI8Zb-MCDf94XpZzr4RETOIayYquukzz-ktj-vMt-CwIW5gSRV6HeUNzTj7yz9of363isWT45lUQoQaI79CGJO48xS5bU6lNO7Co_pOCh0gX3TteLGg-apHTIP382pOZmpecHu8eCF__s2NA9nkil5n3CWBxQcNgWZWTCbJ05U_VkEdc_Z0mBXD0BNPBg_Ovk_EmUjdS1iCBerOj7nRH0GbeTXdEwaMLbE-j5NDHBTPiyp5ImU5xUmvfdoSI2O-VODlI95HkMOReQXb4fOY4jnC2-cFmIm1M4xONp3wvp7XVZuwkgkYSQlxqTKJvVcD26fllG5wgQy51zFqHpLrTAoHW0dDKWaEIq351Knu1dnNp5i1OyYUhBPg0PRychSOK2gG_ljcnQLScakOsK8PKb0yX6MQSocey45kYFkjrd2Iq96i-q73O1ro96MZ0jBRjF3GvDZabAl0-euLdAwFMV9lIszuDFPIHXAt1UgcgaQ0fupDXGMzsl26hkVmRRE)
The liberal "journalists" at the _Times_ just don't understand Black Republicans, so they build their stories about our Senate race around quotes like this one:

| _**"Mr. Walker was merely a vessel for the G.O.P. and Mr. Trump's ambitions."**_
---

And this:

_**"He's a puppet on a string, and somebody's pulling those strings really good."**_
---

**That's absolutely ridiculous.**

Let me be clear about something: I'm Herschel Walker, and I make my own decisions. Nobody tells me what to do.

**[I need your help to defend myself from these insane attacks.](http://links.stoppingsocialistdems.com/u/click?_t=4dcc2aab65fd4d0da6a107b0eef86d43&_m=0f3f1c5f5ca64ce9badeb6b5d542c484&_e=HLWI8Zb-MCDf94XpZzr4RETOIayYquukzz-ktj-vMt-CwIW5gSRV6HeUNzTj7yz9of363isWT45lUQoQaI79CGJO48xS5bU6lNO7Co_pOCh0gX3TteLGg-apHTIP382pOZmpecHu8eCF__s2NA9nkil5n3CWBxQcNgWZWTCbJ05U_VkEdc_Z0mBXD0BNPBg_Ovk_EmUjdS1iCBerOj7nRH0GbeTXdEwaMLbE-j5NDHBTPiyp5ImU5xUmvfdoSI2O-VODlI95HkMOReQXb4fOY7ryF8GXT-ZR7u1Grx9G2_sltAAZ99DlWqI3p6Hffah5K2ncme4bmEpERI9y4XqZImHCaBKq4Xtf9wS1ihBlsvcJa-xgFZ-tHJBa8J6NGR7NoCCg4kdIgvZUwMA9Iv5HtnT3PVUOKlepnHkBeXm9EV86Oonn0x4-M1m_h-WV3gAosqIaX1nSCK6DgoOOAltmAbDI4yvWAh7bldVEAHAwGYuwqqakKL6Yc3pKXmoAnF8v)**

**I'm running for the Senate because Raphael Warnock is taking our country in the wrong direction and does not represent Georgia values.** I'm running to unite Americans and get our nation back on track before it's too late.

It's becoming clearer by the day the Democrats and the liberal media are going to do everything in their power to distract voters from real issues and the disastrous Biden agenda. After all, if this race is a referendum on our unstable economy, record-high prices of groceries, unsecure border, or catastrophic pullout of troops from Afghanistan, Democrats will lose...and they know it.

clean_body: The New York Times is out with another hit piece that focuses on my skin color instead of the issues important in this Senate race. | Peter, The race-based attacks against me just keep coming. As much as we try to focus this campaign on the issues that matter to voters like gas prices, record-high inflation, and crime, the Democrats and their allies in the media only want to talk about race. Check out this recent headline from The New York Times : The liberal "journalists" at the Times just don't understand Black Republicans, so they build their stories about our Senate race around quotes like this one: | "Mr. Walker was merely a vessel for the G.O.P. and Mr. Trump's ambitions." And this: "He's a puppet on a string, and somebody's pulling those strings really good." That's absolutely ridiculous. Let me be clear about something: I'm Herschel Walker, and I make my own decisions. Nobody tells me what to do. I'm running for the Senate because Raphael Warnock is taking our country in the wrong direction and does not represent Georgia values. I'm running to unite Americans and get our nation back on track before it's too late. It's becoming clearer by the day the Democrats and the liberal media are going to do everything in their power to distract voters from real issues and the disastrous Biden agenda. After all, if this race is a referendum on our unstable economy, record-high prices of groceries, unsecure border, or catastrophic pullout of troops from Afghanistan, Democrats will lose...and they know it. Your donation will help us ensure that voters understand who I am and what I stand for. If we're going to win this race, IT'S CRITICAL WE GO ON OFFENSE AND FOCUS THIS RACE ON ISSUES - NOT DISTRACTIONS. Please me stand up to these attacks. I am honored to have you in my corner. Thank you, Herschel Walker Republican Nominee for Senate | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: We'll keep this quick
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, we’ll keep
this quick so you can go enjoy the rest of your Memorial Day.

We’ve had an action-packed May. First, we defeated the Super PACs and
special interests when Summer Lee won her primary in PA-12. Then, we faced
the Democratic establishment head-on in Jessica’s Cisneros’ primary to
challenge anti-abortion, NRA-backed Democrat Henry Cuellar.

Here’s the deal: On holiday weekends, most folks are enjoying time with
loved ones, relaxing, and definitely not checking their email inboxes.
That’s how it should be, and we hope you had a great weekend! But it does
have an impact on the digital fundraising we rely on to power our movement
— especially at a time when we’re still helping Jessica ensure every vote
is counted in TX-28.

We’re preparing for a potential recount in Jessica’s race and facing an
end-of-month fundraising goal. That is why we’re asking: [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/110125?refcode=em20220530-23613&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i ]Can you chip
in a few bucks right now to make up for the holiday slowdown, and to help
us meet our May online fundraising goal?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/110125?refcode=em20220530-23613&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i ]Contribute

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1022&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1023&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20220530-23613-footer&t=1024&refcode2=23613_1203867_3mnK0i&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=23613%2E1203867%2E3mnK0i

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

body:

| | [History is against us, team. The party that controls the White House almost always loses seats during the midterms, and the GOP just needs a net gain of 7 seats to win back the House Majority in November.

name: Josh Weil for US Senate
subject: Taking a Knee on Memorial Day
body:
Monday, May 30, 2022 | Today's the day when every other politician is wishing you a “Happy Memorial Day,” but this year, I refus
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://www.joshweil.org/so/54O4Mid6N/c?w=zwEWQOUJtGtr7F929PKyJdmZLCItranQx-2CffBMddA.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuam9zaHdlaWwub3JnL3NvLzU0TzRNaWQ2Tj9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6Ijg5ZmI1NTQ5LTQ5MzQtNGM0OS1iZDRmLTRhZWEyZTg2MzgxNiJ9)_**
---
| | |
---
| | Josh Weil - Democrat for U.S. Senate (FL)
---
| |
---
| | Monday, May 30, 2022
---
| | |
---
| | Today's the day when every other politician is wishing you a “Happy Memorial Day,” but this year, I refuse. I’ve reached a breaking point, and it’s due in no small part to the response I’ve been witnessing to the controversy surrounding Gabe Kapler, Manager of the San Francisco Giants. Kapler is a man who has begun protesting the national anthem at his workplace because he can no longer show unwavering support for a nation that refuses to address the horrific weight of the gun violence it continues to suffer beneath. The pressure put on him to conform, and to come out for the national anthem, specifically on Memorial Day, has highlighted for me that Memorial Day is truly nothing more than one more disingenuous attempt at placation via “thoughts and prayers.” To be offended that someone like Gabe Kapler wants to focus on the innocent lives being sacrificed to gun violence through our government’s inaction on a day whose intention is to focus on the lives voluntarily sacrificed to preserve this nation for the next generation is farcical at best, and sickening at worst. Memorial Day was never intended to be a bank holiday with burgers and fireworks, it is an essential reminder of a legacy of Americans who paid the ultimate sacrifice to ensure that their children would never have to, and yet today not only do we continue to dishonor their sacrifice the other 364 days of the year with the absolute bare minimum in services and securities for the families of those lost, we continue to allow large gun manufacturers and their lobbyists to spend hundreds of millions of dollars ensuring that capital gains across their industry will continue to be propped up and inflated despite life after life being taken in regularly occurring domestic terrorist attacks at our supermarkets, places of worships, and our children’s schools. We live in a nation where our freely elected democratic government has become enslaved to the military industrial complex, our Senators and Representatives are unable to stand up to the NRA and the gun lobby and pass Common Sense gun reform; to ban private ownership of assault weapons, or even to pass mandatory training and licensing measures-- in order to drive a car in this nation citizens must pass two forms of training exams, meet regular licensing requirements, AND pay mandatory monthly insurance premiums --none of which is required to own a military grade weapon capable of murdering dozens of people in a matter of minutes. If you want me to celebrate Veteran’s Day again, then help me improve our Veteran’s Affairs services to ensure that no veteran in this country ever finds themself without a roof over their head or a meal on the table. Help me to ensure that every single returning combatant receives the intensive mental health and trauma care that they need to live a safe and healthy life back home. If you want me to celebrate Memorial Day again, then let’s use the resources we have to ensure that the families of all of those who were lost in foreign combat are taken care of, that they forever have access to health care, education ,and housing. The indescribable loss, what was taken from their family, can never be replaced. If we want Memorial Day to mean something, then we must be committed to truly giving everything we can to pay these impossible debts, not just one day a year of “thoughts and prayers.” If our elected officials continue to refuse to take action, then I’m with Gabe, and I’m going to have to sit this one out.
Joshua WeilDemocrat for U.S. Senate (FL)
---

name: Adam Schiff
subject: My primary election is next week
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR CONGRESS
body:
| I'll be as brief as possible because we have a major fundraising deadline less than 36 hours from now.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=293%2E5257348%2EZojphj)
Derek,
I'll be as brief as possible because we have a major fundraising deadline less than 36 hours from now.
**My primary election is next week** , and we’re prepping for our final push to canvass the new district, knock on doors, and mobilize voters before the polls close.
However, we're not quite where we need to be with our fundraising goals this month for our campaign and the other good Democrats we are supporting nationwide, and there's very little time for us to make up ground. I'm hoping you can chip in a few bucks to help us reach our goal — it would mean a great deal to me.
Whether you can pitch in $1, $10, or $50, I want you to know that every dollar goes toward winning this primary and helping hold onto our House majority this November.
**If you can, please chip in here:[secure.actblue.com/donate/may2022_abs_em](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/may2022_abs_em?refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC_20220530_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&refcode2=293_5257348_Zojphj&akid=293%2E5257348%2EZojphj)**
Thank you for stepping up and supporting my campaign in this final stretch.
Onward to primary day,
— Adam
150 E. Olive Ave., Suite 208 Burbank CA, 91502
**[www.adamschiff.com](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=2&akid=293%2E5257348%2EZojphj) | [contact@adamschiff.com](mailto:contact@adamschiff.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=3&akid=293%2E5257348%2EZojphj)**

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=2699%2E878179%2EoC5ECA). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=2699%2E878179%2EoC5ECA).**

| | As we pause to recognize, acknowledge, mourn, celebrate and pay gratitude to the those who made the ultimate sacrifice for this great country and its citizens, we also thank and offer encouragement and gratitude to their surviving families and friends who have endured great sacrifice as they have navigated life without those heroes. It is such immeasurable sacrifice that has enabled our country to progress to this point and allowed us to live the idea of sovereignty with liberty and freedom that we enjoy each and every day. It is incumbent upon us individually and collectively to honor those sacrifices by continuing to fight for our freedoms, and by living our lives with respect of our fellow citizens, their opinions, freedoms, ideas and individual right to pursue happiness. With this respect we become more united and, thus, more powerful in our honor of those who laid down their lives for us to freely live ours. Have a safe and blessed Memorial Day! Be Blessed,
Dr. Myron L. Hall
info@drhallforsenate.com

If we don’t take down Fetterman, we can kiss the Senate majority, and America, goodbye. Fellow American,

The race-based attacks against me just keep coming.

As much as we try to focus this campaign on the issues that matter to voters like gas prices, record-high inflation, and crime, the Democrats and their allies in the media only want to talk about race.

Check out this recent headline from _The New York Times_ :

[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c4f3238a35d947ecafbe0eece2e1c06e&_e=qtiKaS15oGCWr26gH5WmBoWxfkslgOJRTKSmEsHkIy5rTFFABzEBOExmf0zbPcJjdtNu_EO1Wib5zHgBhMSXo9l3crf_T3XFIgFMktt8U2q5YWdwZYp-_SVTUmjBn7CA7y7ctBP8ImnCA5H31dPcUaumycYXNrVvxnWF-gpLs3G6DWQp-sodyFQUT1VkxNV83fDBtVHFHjEa3ZYa4TJNi5Qt2nWCWB1t-VMx9t_8olXDYaQR-qSmH0vuxKhSxSBagRH7Bz0ZeA5t-PArnQibeiePnEGeKgMNkDfOL5VvhklL2GAtEHxLCOahRabTp5HbAy3qoseiT1DkMVC50Qxu2ooRbUcA7BZdSG_6bBpZCWb7n57WWlmyJzX2zONe_wDlkovzmEbXmxj6svoNTusr3TFHf3Fzg2GLior80yXbvJCg3efZm8e9mBlmBvT4AF2pOao1p3OsY835rhk72GsPTtE0qRgmHwxwdpPu9hBi_rI%3D)
The liberal "journalists" at the _Times_ just don't understand Black Republicans, so they build their stories about our Senate race around quotes like this one:

| _**"Mr. Walker was merely a vessel for the G.O.P. and Mr. Trump's ambitions."**_
---

And this:

_**"He's a puppet on a string, and somebody's pulling those strings really good."**_
---

**That's absolutely ridiculous.**

Let me be clear about something: I'm Herschel Walker, and I make my own decisions. Nobody tells me what to do.

**[I need your help to defend myself from these insane attacks.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=c4f3238a35d947ecafbe0eece2e1c06e&_e=qtiKaS15oGCWr26gH5WmBoWxfkslgOJRTKSmEsHkIy5rTFFABzEBOExmf0zbPcJjdtNu_EO1Wib5zHgBhMSXo9l3crf_T3XFIgFMktt8U2q5YWdwZYp-_SVTUmjBn7CA7y7ctBP8ImnCA5H31dPcUaumycYXNrVvxnWF-gpLs3G6DWQp-sodyFQUT1VkxNV83fDBtVHFHjEa3ZYa4TJNi5Qt2nWCWB1t-VMx9t_8olXDYaQR-qSmH0vuxKhSxSBagRH7Bz0ZeA5t-PArnQibesa8qhT649vDJ29rWtjr2-bWhE-EwDn8KN2pZRr0ol85Uy-0OHHPBJ4OqJO9xXUXyYRZe-T5u72qxs2dDkassj0y1DC641EUK068GNXNItkZnZvixd5n64_3b1c8V3ZLN29IJINje2_WPBus9Nyi1eyV1R4xSgqWBOdprDuKA0Y9ux11cMf237gj-i-AwM95xCh3Rs0y1ZkcF_cglB3Wnag%3D)**

clean_body: The New York Times is out with another hit piece that focuses on my skin color instead of the issues important in this Senate race. | Fellow American, The race-based attacks against me just keep coming. As much as we try to focus this campaign on the issues that matter to voters like gas prices, record-high inflation, and crime, the Democrats and their allies in the media only want to talk about race. Check out this recent headline from The New York Times : The liberal "journalists" at the Times just don't understand Black Republicans, so they build their stories about our Senate race around quotes like this one: | "Mr. Walker was merely a vessel for the G.O.P. and Mr. Trump's ambitions." And this: "He's a puppet on a string, and somebody's pulling those strings really good." That's absolutely ridiculous. Let me be clear about something: I'm Herschel Walker, and I make my own decisions. Nobody tells me what to do. I'm running for the Senate because Raphael Warnock is taking our country in the wrong direction and does not represent Georgia values. I'm running to unite Americans and get our nation back on track before it's too late. It's becoming clearer by the day the Democrats and the liberal media are going to do everything in their power to distract voters from real issues and the disastrous Biden agenda. After all, if this race is a referendum on our unstable economy, record-high prices of groceries, unsecure border, or catastrophic pullout of troops from Afghanistan, Democrats will lose...and they know it. Your donation will help us ensure that voters understand who I am and what I stand for. If we're going to win this race, IT'S CRITICAL WE GO ON OFFENSE AND FOCUS THIS RACE ON ISSUES - NOT DISTRACTIONS. Please me stand up to these attacks. I am honored to have you in my corner. Thank you, Herschel Walker Republican Nominee for Senate | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

name: Shahid Buttar
subject: What Memorial Day means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shahid Buttar for Congress Committee, 1390 Market St
body:
Memorial Day offers a poignant annual reminder of the costs of militarism, not only to veterans who never made it home, but also those who survived, their families, and the communities deprived of resources hoarded by the bipartisan military-industrial co | | |
---
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yRkW_Sw2xr1MvIXEt6jooTbMnsVYqhUO8Xwt4ORz78ORT8Pt3SALA3pMGVDypeKbyy2kgsjsCmnmy8k7FgsqB6wuCJ9K2SYervuUQSBWz0Ow4RaxiGVvqPNPMoG2M3fDNGyUm9KHYewDeaiBFbnSfs2j3NKqanxi0O5hVAnbe4etc-PG2JHP39QKgAirUv-7FcnWxeVZP-7_tawvV2xCp8OLv5ubUdrHZaZmeCoY24HZ4lO0WsY3oMdFCwnRwQIFl9B4zU20_4EO_5P8hDOh0Ylhl78HxE8IJkzyh3_7S28G7TJwJz1rg879rmkCUM2OKSFpJfVMFZXrXzMDoqyUXSyw0yHDTsMFAhvlQHzvnk3A/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h0/VmHuUy2r6Hcf06Setc75OuShf7OegldsW3kA23OuH_o)
| | Dear Peter, Amidst the barbecues and family outings of Memorial Day weekend, I hope you can spare a moment to consider today’s significance. It’s a day to not only recall the sacrifices of fallen veterans, but also to consider our shared social responsibilities to servicemembers, their families, and international human rights. [**Can you join us this holiday weekend to honor veterans by working to stop the next war before it starts?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MIa9Y6y0KlqELJiGJZ3AgEENdN9wuvZms6gmL_0Uk-NaBSffa16Sop8PiUf0ByB1R73zHbOS1lW_c816A2QuUIZ9rlBJGWMK-cGWj1VOgu5tnR5iKqBW1lW_6iXc76Y-_GAi2nPlsceyXBMknCIBcuPe8NiCLL0wqEkZ0QexxX_w2yxj93zpkwb7-EL_GDGT95VqilvyQ8tPzraErQikZE44mitV4W9Lz3gwTuzfoM1D6t9uamqqBG-2FUk6e4qb3Ax2aPNsPMBAgXTbBL52lco/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h1/sGt5aYAtX1xPBFXb9zJT_zUpJMS3mXcmcskRA3BYYbA) Both of my older brothers served in the Reserve Officers Training Corps. One was eventually discharged for medical reasons, while the other served in the U.S. Army Medical Corps for several years, deployed in various locations from South Carolina to South Korea. I’m glad he made it home at the end of his deployments. He was one of the lucky ones. Too many other veterans come home with shattered bodies, or minds torn by memories of what they’ve seen, or families challenged by burdens like remote parenthood or living in constant fear of a loved one’s safety. [**We’re working on Memorial Day weekend to challenge an oligarch with a long history of complicity in military industrial corruption. Can you join us today to put the lives of veterans before the profits of weapons manufacturers?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MIa9Y6y0KlqELJiGJZ3AgEENdN9wuvZms6gmL_0Uk-NaH7GhxAXPkrppMFjRC6sdIwDwrWvTARGoqkfIWcj4blpThF7ae1yZDk2xzZsYeiTIb4tDKAHicTx0lZZt6NdKASEq1YPOQ8OLMt2cKK9U4tq5ZMT0RHOHRVYqaSKMGNn6yPbqrCwCd1rX-3eukqc4Vveq2ALy6TxHeQaSrIMIq1r1ynfksG3qHrwg-ZKwsJ4xmko8SGT3C65AcFf4XU8mZ0nnWXB8qKui5VtyiVaLp_W/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h2/MMe8ecqcyChHPKiAqp4ZViZlYvTf3FwNs5W29Ej4P4k) Veterans sign up to defend the United States. But time after time, a bipartisan consensus in Washington emerges to place them at grave risk not to defend our country, but instead to extend Wall Street’s interests. That has proven to be the case in every conflict since the Second World War. Veterans are not expendable salespeople for weapons contracts. Yet, that’s how Washington treats them, whichever party is in the White House. They deserve civilian leadership that reflects the enormity of their sacrifice, and treats them with the respect they deserve—not just in words, but in action. [**Can you support our campaign to secure that leadership, by sending to Congress a voice that has challenged militarism, championed transparency, and called out warmongers in both of the major political parties?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MIa9Y6y0KlqELJiGJZ3AgEENdN9wuvZms6gmL_0Uk-NaHRMJdwxd2D2K1bBHiFg8OXqdSnNnqcQcYu96XqBYw86NNaGvKRN_fIV6-h_W91ZfgMcbh_SWIKpGypfLmG-7zaxY9USKJIV88sWflAworhFgiCgVkQnMobL8BBqt6uxuGujjUatodGv7-mUnY-xpebn-plo2yRdbZIQgFTeKoTwCdMhYIe58d0HOR6fecQJhoyyizP3ltKEexxWcQsoelyr1MWdVhZnIM3RnnygRy26/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h3/ovUMz4eO6VQWv4-s89BtErg0Xv2VYj4l0wYg_w1BfgI) I hope to walk in the footsteps of the late U.S. Sen. Maurice “Mike” Gravel (D-AK). He endorsed our campaign to replace Nancy Pelosi in 2020 before he passed away, long after helping bring down the Nixon administration, ending the war on Vietnam, and running for the White House in 2008. Sen. Gravel understood something that many of his colleagues never learn. Congress holds a constitutional responsibility not only to legislate policy, but also—crucially—to oversee, check, and balance the executive branch. Military spending alone demands more aggressive oversight. Congressional failure to conduct that oversight is precisely what enabled the escalating corruption that pervades the contracting process, as well as the continuing history of unaccountable human rights violations by U.S. intelligence agencies. That’s ultimately why I’m running for Congress, rather than a local position in government: I can’t fight the CIA, or stop the next war, or support the next Pentagon whistleblower, from City Hall. And that’s ultimately what drove me into electoral politics. **[Our primary election looms in just one week, on June 7. Can you join us today to hold accountable a powerful oligarch who mouths support for veterans and their families after having helped repeatedly place them at risk for Wall Street?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTCxhAMhTRXK4t_BbI_sQ5MIa9Y6y0KlqELJiGJZ3AgEENdN9wuvZms6gmL_0Uk-NaBZgv2NMkRuF3VSgPt84gr60zeH746AK4lOapvj9B6CGDuvbYNUtBECPyQD47FovhvMaojE4FK7bgEM6NQuvEBSo3BXe1XzjDXfXb339A3bM4fskzy9rLB4XLHcWJX1dgeDcynvxhV_6o1MXMJhHi64c938JhzF2TRy5DKo6I0Gf9FiE43O3XRSjYJ5it0Kw9f4l6IC-2xweU0xSukHJVPxwNzbCNuiqiYF1PZx1q3Xi/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h4/P1657gIZnOfB8us6yT-wwgTFLpWU10Y7KeDy9AXNr5g) ** With just one week remaining before the jungle primary ends, we’re working hard to reach every voter we can to offer an alternative. Your support helps us work around our obstacles, including a press establishment that cares more about tabloid clickbait—like a story you might have seen this weekend about a member of Pelosi’s family overlooking her substantial corruption—than adversarial journalism. Thank you for standing with us! I hope you & your family enjoy a pleasant holiday weekend—and that together, we can honor fallen veterans and their families by stopping the next war before it starts to keep more people alive. Your voice,
Shahid PS -- Want to help, but can't contribute financially? Your time can make a big difference from wherever you live! [Sign up here to volunteer with our campaign](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTNyy0c_kMvO1sM-_6WbdMUGxIpBTUsHX1e8FJrj-biEYDOFiZiO1PPnjeCpC1OrMhVl6aLvHT1ytYa40bQad55GoLn2vYiccwXXOXpGMMyqENngfR4ZWjKC2AcIOZIA5U8P2F4-AyXkyK58SmOW1C-LDDE2YxKnkcerfo0MaLwHcYU2jmkPaJxzeR9jQI0CUWdXL7dhPZqE9BcQMWumHYoAu4l8CLdFbA9_y-Wx0olg6Q0Zq5jQyeN0ihJyC4xTl04lc9MCnrYM3j32sGHr5vVE/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h5/uZXnQPHikYC53ZoADfO08zB2Fe3aEBJxfLN0sptS410) to end the era of bipartisan corruption in Washington, and share this email with friends who share our concerns about militarism. |
---|---|---
|
---
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDPAZiEt8wwS6hp5wOIb3CiK3QYPf9O2YAvIrWYttWwfKt1dO0HbqxlxW2MOzEKVR5955iRjYiVjXpPCtiTLxcqy4c6aoepdUGqsyAgM_0CCq2UdyujlReRPq3yhkBv1qhdytvepAOZwCKSS9OBaJqQngKRz_LLU-DGckajjtNC1f8cX84TstoIuUCtj7GJSZ8OWkMr7UBZ1Ft2iR_KD2Ec4UdarJOQzyfzgUiyOcmCHLtAwbV7okYHUW25zyPV8Nyn-ZGiw40kvRY8c2MlMi0wxgI5vbnmh3Rzyr2UPuB-oM/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h6/vfdiuZqRUJLS5hJ8MxCP5SDBR1d45TXdLjGSguyR4QE) [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/dSCQfi9FLISmU3ZE3bfPhqFPbIOt_SSei2w6SESbB97ywZMbmRV0RLmqyLIOr_bb3nB2iypzbnBdZu_CRHt4dHyrNBtm65CpXYcCh8ZIL0KxcHe2EKNpf8ocmF5w6MjAiYJYseqx2areRsTzOTyujEmKy04l2LU7pCYQo8-yBs2JkviGL2Gt1Y0QLhhKlnlsHUZ-af2249SXL4TSeoWmxnUm1jU4qTZAMMxWcYSJ5AuXq_CwGcdOpRIcFTxvCQNvXlhvAcdp8g-fEGYz7GG_7nFdImN4qMvU9luROK6qOBM/3mh/hP2VVretQZSgDxIOy-Xfkw/h7/vDoP5_MdGFfg9AlvrO2iOmBhYRGAZzyqdMxjks0NWJo)

clean_body: Memorial Day offers a poignant annual reminder of the costs of militarism, not only to veterans who never made it home, but also those who survived, their families, and the communities deprived of resources hoarded by the bipartisan military-industrial co | | | | | Dear Peter, Amidst the barbecues and family outings of Memorial Day weekend, I hope you can spare a moment to consider today’s significance. It’s a day to not only recall the sacrifices of fallen veterans, but also to consider our shared social responsibilities to servicemembers, their families, and international human rights. Both of my older brothers served in the Reserve Officers Training Corps. One was eventually discharged for medical reasons, while the other served in the U.S. Army Medical Corps for several years, deployed in various locations from South Carolina to South Korea. I’m glad he made it home at the end of his deployments. He was one of the lucky ones. Too many other veterans come home with shattered bodies, or minds torn by memories of what they’ve seen, or families challenged by burdens like remote parenthood or living in constant fear of a loved one’s safety. Veterans sign up to defend the United States. But time after time, a bipartisan consensus in Washington emerges to place them at grave risk not to defend our country, but instead to extend Wall Street’s interests. That has proven to be the case in every conflict since the Second World War. Veterans are not expendable salespeople for weapons contracts. Yet, that’s how Washington treats them, whichever party is in the White House. They deserve civilian leadership that reflects the enormity of their sacrifice, and treats them with the respect they deserve—not just in words, but in action. I hope to walk in the footsteps of the late U.S. Sen. Maurice “Mike” Gravel (D-AK). He endorsed our campaign to replace Nancy Pelosi in 2020 before he passed away, long after helping bring down the Nixon administration, ending the war on Vietnam, and running for the White House in 2008. Sen. Gravel understood something that many of his colleagues never learn. Congress holds a constitutional responsibility not only to legislate policy, but also—crucially—to oversee, check, and balance the executive branch. Military spending alone demands more aggressive oversight. Congressional failure to conduct that oversight is precisely what enabled the escalating corruption that pervades the contracting process, as well as the continuing history of unaccountable human rights violations by U.S. intelligence agencies. That’s ultimately why I’m running for Congress, rather than a local position in government: I can’t fight the CIA, or stop the next war, or support the next Pentagon whistleblower, from City Hall. And that’s ultimately what drove me into electoral politics. With just one week remaining before the jungle primary ends, we’re working hard to reach every voter we can to offer an alternative. Your support helps us work around our obstacles, including a press establishment that cares more about tabloid clickbait—like a story you might have seen this weekend about a member of Pelosi’s family overlooking her substantial corruption—than adversarial journalism. Thank you for standing with us! I hope you & your family enjoy a pleasant holiday weekend—and that together, we can honor fallen veterans and their families by stopping the next war before it starts to keep more people alive. Your voice, Shahid PS -- Want to help, but can't contribute financially? Your time can make a big difference from wherever you live! to end the era of bipartisan corruption in Washington, and share this email with friends who share our concerns about militarism. | ---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Shahid Buttar for Congress Committee, 1390 Market St., Suite 200, San Francisco, CA 94102 | | Sent via . ---
Dear Peter,

Many years ago, my father told me about his experiences fighting during the Battle of the Bulge.

My Dad spoke glowingly about General Omar Bradley and many of the fellow soldiers who he fought alongside with during some of the most difficult days of that battle.

At one point in the conversation, my Dad said to me he was a very lucky man because he got a million dollar wound when he was fighting. He was shot through the calf earning a Purple Heart, but while off the line for some time, he considered himself very fortunate.

He then talked about his friends who didn’t make it and how many people he knew who died giving everything for our country.

On this Memorial Day, we honor the memory of those who gave everything to our nation. I also thank my Dad and Mom for teaching me about the men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice.

It’s a beautiful day here in Charleston, West Virginia. Today, I can’t help but think of all of the families who have lived through so much hardship after one of their family members didn’t return home from war.

Enjoy the rest of the weekend. I will always remember their sacrifice and do my best to honor their memories.
Sincerely,

clean_body: | | | | We just received word that a Louisiana U.S. District Court has issued an injunction... Dear Peter, Many years ago, my father told me about his experiences fighting during the Battle of the Bulge. My Dad spoke glowingly about General Omar Bradley and many of the fellow soldiers who he fought alongside with during some of the most difficult days of that battle. At one point in the conversation, my Dad said to me he was a very lucky man because he got a million dollar wound when he was fighting. He was shot through the calf earning a Purple Heart, but while off the line for some time, he considered himself very fortunate. He then talked about his friends who didn’t make it and how many people he knew who died giving everything for our country. On this Memorial Day, we honor the memory of those who gave everything to our nation. I also thank my Dad and Mom for teaching me about the men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice. It’s a beautiful day here in Charleston, West Virginia. Today, I can’t help but think of all of the families who have lived through so much hardship after one of their family members didn’t return home from war. Enjoy the rest of the weekend. I will always remember their sacrifice and do my best to honor their memories. Sincerely, Patrick Morrisey West Virginia Attorney General | Follow us on Social Media ****|****|****|****|** Paid for by TEAM MORRISEY, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit of Morrisey for Attorney General, Morrisey for West Virginia, Inc., Blue and Gold Fund, and West Virginia Republican Party, Inc. c/o Red Curve Solutions 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor Beverly, MA 01915.
